documents.text: Daniel Zachary M. Mercurio is a Software Developer. Computer engineer and software developer from Subic, Zambales building end-to-end systems across full-stack development, databases, computer vision, LLMs, RAG, machine learning, and automation. Experienced with industrial AI inspection, augmented-reality PCB tooling, local and cloud LLM support systems, VBA workflow automation, QGIS mapping, and portfolio-scale web development. Key highlights: Lead developer for AI-powered computer vision AOI system at Wistron Infocomm; Built full-stack AI systems with board capture, local processing, custom vision models, and industrial-grade inspection datasets; Reduced motherboard inspection time by 150% and motherboard net tracing time by 60%; Developed augmented reality board schematic tooling for PCB design, analysis, board tracking, and accuracy; Built local and cloud-based LLM RAG support over proprietary company data; Completed BS Computer Engineering with GWA 1.66 and Best in Thesis Award.
I build practical interfaces around developer and operator workflows, using web technologies such as HTML, CSS, JavaScript, React, Vue, Angular, and Shopify when the goal is a usable product surface.
I approach backend work as the system layer that makes automation useful: databases, APIs, local processing, PHP/R/SQL tooling, and data-backed workflows that reduce manual effort.
I build AI and computer vision systems from data capture through model training and deployment, including OpenCV, YOLO, CNNs, OpenVINO, MLOps, edge AI, and annotation pipelines.
I look for repeated work that can become software: VBA Excel automation, QGIS mapping updates, database cleanup, job order processing, and internal support tools.

Daniel Zachary M. Mercurio completed Bachelor of Science in Computer Engineering at President Ramon Magsaysay State University in Iba, Zambales, Philippines from 2021 - 2025. Best in Thesis Award recipient; major in Software Development; thesis/project work included DenseNet-121 powered rice leaf disease identification. Coursework included Software Development; Computer Engineering; Machine Learning; Database Management; CAD and Design; Embedded Systems; Web Development. Details: GWA: 1.66; Best in Thesis Award, June 23, 2025.

metadata.degree: Bachelor of Science in Computer Engineering

documents.text: Daniel completed STEM Graduate at Saint James School of Subic Inc. from 2019 - 2021. Highlights: Grade 11 With Honors; Grade 12 With High Honors.

metadata.school: Saint James School of Subic Inc.

documents.text: Daniel's domains include: Software Development; AI/ML Engineering; Computer Vision; Industrial Automation; Full Stack Development; Database Management; Edge AI; MLOps; CAD and Design; Embedded Systems.
Daniel's languages include: C++; Python; C#; Java; Dart; Rust; Arduino; MicroPython; HTML; CSS; JavaScript; PHP; R; SQL; VBA.

Daniel's ai ml include: OpenCV; YOLO; YOLOv8; CNN; DenseNet-121; OpenVINO; LLM; RAG; Agentic AI; MLOps; Edge AI; TensorFlow; Kaggle; Jupyter Notebook; Self-supervised learning; Knowledge distillation.
Daniel's frameworks include: React; Node.js; Vue; Angular; Flutter; Next.js.
Daniel's databases include: SQL; NoSQL; PHP-backed database tooling; R-based data workflows.

Daniel's tools include: Git; GitHub; Docker; Figma; Power BI; Kaggle; Jupyter; QGIS; Microsoft Word; Microsoft Excel; Microsoft Publisher; Microsoft PowerPoint; Proteus; AutoCAD; EasyEDA; Blender; SketchUp; Shopify.
Daniel's core stack include: Python; OpenCV; YOLO; OpenVINO; LLM; RAG; SQL; React; Node.js; Docker; Git; QGIS; VBA.

Daniel worked as Software/Automations Engineer at Wistron Infocomm, Phil. from 2025 - Present as full-time. Highlights: Lead developer of AI-powered computer vision AOI system; Engineered a full-stack AI system including board capture, local processing, and custom vision models; Streamlined the AI data pipeline with cleaned and annotated datasets for industrial-grade inspection; Reduced motherboard inspection time by 150%; Showcased AI inspection work to international customers including HP, Dell, and Zebra; Optimized computer vision system to run on edge devices; Managed end-to-end data pipeline including collection, cleaning, and precision annotation for model training; Led augmented reality board schematic system development; Reduced motherboard net tracing time by 60%; Developed PCB design and analysis tooling with optimized board tracking and accuracy; Developed company LLM RAG system with local and cloud-based support over proprietary company data.

Daniel worked as IT and Administrative Work - OJT at ZAMECO II from June - July 2024 as on-the-job training. Highlights: Completed 240 hours of on-the-job training; Implemented VBA automation scripts for Excel sheets with 30% productivity increase; Increased productivity of job order completion by 30%; Managed and sorted four years of backlogged data and job orders; Implemented updated mapping technology using QGIS; Improved efficiency of administrative tasks and workplace environment.

Project: AI-Powered Computer Vision AOI System. Full-stack industrial inspection system with board capture, local processing, cleaned and annotated datasets, and custom vision models optimized for edge devices. Reduced motherboard inspection time by 150% and was showcased to international customers. Category: AI/ML. Skills used: Python; OpenCV; YOLO; CNN; OpenVINO; Edge AI; MLOps; Data Annotation. Status or date: production. Link: not specified.

Project: Augmented Reality Board Schematic System. PCB design and analysis tool for AR-assisted board schematic workflows. Optimized board tracking and accuracy while reducing motherboard net tracing time by 60%. Category: Production. Skills used: Computer Vision; Augmented Reality; Python; PCB Analysis; CAD. Status or date: production. Link: not specified.

Project: Company LLM RAG System. Local and cloud-based LLM support system using retrieval augmented generation over proprietary company data. Category: AI/ML. Skills used: LLM; RAG; Agentic AI; Databases; Cloud; Local AI. Status or date: internal. Link: not specified.

Project: DenseNet-121 Powered Rice Leaf Disease Identifier. Machine learning project using DenseNet-121 for rice leaf disease identification. Category: AI/ML. Skills used: Python; DenseNet-121; TensorFlow; Kaggle; Jupyter Notebook; Image Classification. Status or date: May 2025. Link: not specified.
